title: viola philippica var. pseudojaponica
intro: Viola philippica var. pseudojaponica, also known as Japanese field pansy or varietal pseudo-japanese violet, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Viola philippica var. pseudojaponica include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and possessing potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in tea, tincture, and infusion forms, often used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
